Name: Send Email using MailKit
Description:
This step template supports sending an email using the [MailKit](https://github.com/jstedfast/MailKit) library, a cross-platform .NET library for IMAP, POP3, and SMTP.

- Support for multiple TO addresses (separated by `,`).
- Support for multiple CC addresses (separated by `,`).
- *Optional* support for a separate `reply-to` address.

This step **does not** support running on PowerShell: Windows Desktop Edition.

---

**Required:** 
- The `MailKit` and `MimeKit` packages are installed on the target or worker. If the packages can't be found, the step will attempt to download them from [https://www.nuget.org](https://www.nuget.org).
- PowerShell Core (including when running on Windows).

*Notes:*

- Tested on Octopus `2025.1`.
- Tested on Windows and Linux with PowerShell Core only.

Parameters.Name: SendEmail.SmtpPort
Parameters.Label: SMTP Port
Parameters.HelpText:
The SMTP Port used for connecting. Typical SMTP ports are: 
- `25`
- `587` (STARTTLS)
- `465` (SSL)

Parameters.Name: SendEmail.SecureSocketOption
Parameters.Label: SSL Option
Parameters.HelpText:
The [SSL Option](https://mimekit.net/docs/html/T_MailKit_Security_SecureSocketOptions.htm) to use:
- `None`: No SSL or TLS encryption should be used
- `Auto`: Allow the [IMailService](https://mimekit.net/docs/html/T_MailKit_IMailService.htm) to decide which SSL or TLS options to use (default). If the server does not support SSL or TLS, then the connection will continue without any encryption.
- `SslOnConnect`: The connection should use SSL or TLS encryption immediately.
- `StartTls`: Elevates the connection to use TLS encryption immediately after reading the greeting and capabilities of the server. If the server does not support the STARTTLS extension, then the connection will fail and a NotSupportedException will be thrown.
- `StartTlsWhenAvailable`: Elevates the connection to use TLS encryption immediately after reading the greeting and capabilities of the server, but only if the server supports the STARTTLS extension.

Default: `Auto`.

DisplaySettings.Octopus.SelectOptions:
None|None
Auto|Auto
SslOnConnect|Use SSL
StartTls|Use STARTTLS
StartTlsWhenAvailable|Start Tls When Available

Parameters.Name: SendEmail.Credentials.Username
Parameters.Label: Authentication Username
Parameters.HelpText: The username to authenticate with the SMTP Server.

Parameters.Name: SendEmail.Credentials.Password
Parameters.Label: Authentication Password
Parameters.HelpText: The password to authenticate with the SMTP Server.

Parameters.Name: SendEmail.FromAddress
Parameters.Label: From Address
Parameters.HelpText: The sender address of the Email

Parameters.Name: SendEmail.TOAddresses
Parameters.Label: TO addresses
Parameters.HelpText: The recipients of the Email. Multiple addresses can be supplied, separated by a `,`.

Parameters.Name: SendEmail.CCAddresses
Parameters.Label: (Optional) CC addresses
Parameters.HelpText: The *optional* CC (carbon-copy) recipients of the Email. Multiple addresses can be supplied, separated by a `,`.

Parameters.Name: SendEmail.HtmlBody
Parameters.Label: HTML Message Body
Parameters.HelpText: The HTML message body of the Email.

Parameters.Name: SendEmail.TextBody
Parameters.Label: Plain Text Message Body
Parameters.HelpText: The Text message body of the Email.
